Container.Name: AppFlowy-Certbot
Container.Overview: Optional (TLS): Certbot/ACME automated certificate management for AppFlowy-Nginx. Obtains and renews Let's Encrypt TLS certificates using the HTTP-01 webroot challenge. Part of the AppFlowy Cloud stack.
Container.Requires: AppFlowy-Nginx must be running with nginx-letsencrypt.conf and port 80 accessible from the internet before running this container. Both containers share the letsencrypt and certbot-webroot host directories.
Container.Description:
Certbot (ACME client) for automated Let's Encrypt TLS certificate management. Works as a companion to AppFlowy-Nginx using the HTTP-01 webroot challenge.

Setup steps:
  1. Edit the Extra Parameters field: replace yourdomain.com and admin@yourdomain.com with your actual domain and email.
  2. Make sure AppFlowy-Nginx is running with nginx-letsencrypt.conf and port 80 is reachable from the internet.
  3. Start AppFlowy-Certbot. It will obtain the certificate and exit — this is normal.
  4. Restart AppFlowy-Nginx to load the new certificate.
  5. Schedule AppFlowy-Certbot to restart periodically (e.g. monthly via Unraid User Scripts plugin) to auto-renew. Certbot skips renewal if the cert expires in more than 30 days.

Certificate paths (inside the nginx container):
/etc/letsencrypt/live/yourdomain.com/fullchain.pem
/etc/letsencrypt/live/yourdomain.com/privkey.pem
Update nginx-letsencrypt.conf to replace YOUR_DOMAIN with your actual domain name.

Config.Name: Let's Encrypt data
Config.Description: Persistent storage for Let's Encrypt account keys and certificates. Must match the path mapped in AppFlowy-Nginx.

Config.Name: Certbot webroot
Config.Description: Webroot directory for ACME challenge files. Must match the path mapped in AppFlowy-Nginx.
